channel.title: Internet Society Events
channel.description: Latest events to get involved with Internet Society initiatives

item.title: Taller Online: Blindaje Digital – De las llaves perdidas a la seguridad total
item.description: ¿Sientes que tienes demasiadas contraseñas y terminas usando siempre la misma? ¿Te preocupa quién puede leer tus mensajes o acceder a tus fotos? En este taller práctico y gratuito, aprenderemos a proteger nuestra vida digital de una manera sencilla, sin importar si tienes 15 o 75 años. La seguridad en internet no tiene por qué ser complicada.

item.title: Trust & Safety Online: Encryption and Internet Policy in the Caribbean
item.description: What do new online safety and age verification laws mean for your privacy? Join ISOC Jamaica for a conversation on encryption, the changing regulatory landscape, and how the LAC community can shape a safer internet.

item.title: CLNOG2026
item.description: Reunión de operadores de red de Chile.

item.title: Cifrado y Seguridad en Línea
item.description: "Si el cifrado es fuerte, Internet es fuerte". El objetivo de esta presentación es empoderar a la audiencia para que comprenda que el cifrado es la herramienta técnica que garantiza una sociedad digital democrática y segura.

item.title: Driving Sustainable Change: ISOC Haiti’s Community Network Model in Rivière Salée, Baradères.
item.description: 13:06Led by ISOC Haiti Chapter, this community network in Rivière Salée provides affordable internet to a rural community, supporting education, economic growth, and digital inclusion, sharing lessons learned to inform future initiatives.

item.title: Resiliencia de Internet ante Conflictos y Desastres
item.description: Internet es infraestructura crítica: ante desastres o conflictos, la conectividad puede ser vital. Este webinar explora los tres pilares de resiliencia de Internet Society para mantener redes operativas cuando más se necesitan.

item.title: Why Encryption Matters To You
item.description: This webinar will examine the importance of strong encryption tools for end users and will review potential encryption threats across select countries of the Caribbean.

item.title: Conectividad Centrada en la Comunidad - - Internet segura
item.description: Este evento online enseña cómo mantener tu comunidad conectada de forma segura. Descubre herramientas prácticas para navegar con confianza y proteger tu información, con foco en los desafíos del despliegue de internet en comunidades urbanas y rurales.

item.title: Campaña Neutralidad de la Red
item.description: Generaremos y pondremos a disposición del resto de los capítulos una campaña con una serie de productos gráficos (vídeos e ilustraciones) para generar conciencia sobre la importancia del principio de neutralidad de la red.

item.title: Connecting Rural Suriname: Internet Access for Indigenous and Tribal Peoples for Distance Learning and Digital Inclusion
item.description: This project brings reliable internet access to rural Indigenous and Tribal communities in Suriname, supporting an existing distance learning programme through stable, safe connectivity to enable digital inclusion.

item.title: Más allá del acceso: ¿Qué significa ‘Internet significativo’ en educación?”
item.description: En Perú, la conectividad escolar ha crecido, pero no garantiza aprendizaje ni agencia digital. Este webinar explora qué condiciones pedagógicas, institucionales y tecnológicas convierten el acceso en capacidades reales.

item.title: Student Transition & Elevation Partnership Event
item.description: A networking and career-readiness event for engineering and tech students and young professionals, focused on preparing attendees for the transition from campus to career.

item.title: Guatemala Internet Governance Forum 2026 (IGF Guatemala 2026)
item.description: IGF Guatemala 2026 is a national multistakeholder platform uniting government, academia, private sector, civil society, and the technical community to discuss digital policy, cybersecurity, AI, and emerging technologies, promoting an inclusive internet ecosystem.

item.title: Día Internacional de las Niñas en las TIC 2026 – Guatemala
item.description: International Girls in ICT Day 2026 in Guatemala inspires girls to explore technology careers through keynotes, interactive sessions, and discussions on AI, digital skills, and STEM education, promoting digital inclusion across Latin America and the Caribbean.

item.title: South School on Internet Governance (SSIG) Guatemala 2026
item.description: SSIG Guatemala 2026 strengthens internet governance knowledge across Latin America and the Caribbean, covering cybersecurity, digital policy, infrastructure, and emerging technologies, promoting regional collaboration and participation in governance forums.

item.title: ARGENSIG - Argentina School on Internet Governance
item.description: ARGENSIG trains future internet governance leaders across Latin America — onsite and hybrid — covering the technical, political, economic, social, and legal dimensions of the digital ecosystem, with a focus on the challenges of Argentina and the region.

item.title: Legislación de menores en redes sociales Caso: Costa Rica
item.description: Análisis sobre verificación de edad y la nueva legislación propuesta en Costa Rica para el control de uso de redes sociales en menores de edad.

item.title: Conoce a COMTELCA, Comisión Técnica Regional de Telecomunicaciones de CentroAmérica.
item.description: COMTELCA is SICA's specialized body coordinating telecommunications and ICT across Central America, uniting eight countries through a shared legal framework and binding resolutions.

item.title: Grupo de Mediciones LAC - ISOC Uruguay Chapter
item.description: Webinar organizado por Capítulo ISOC Uruguay para presentar avances del grupo a la comunidad regional.

item.title: Webinar sobre el proyecto MEDICIONES LAC
item.description: Un proyecto colaborativo para entender mediciones en Internet en LAC y trabajar en la implementación del DNSSEC.

item.title: UA TechBoost 2026 LACNOG
item.description: UA TechBoost reúne a líderes de ICANN, UNESCO, ISOC y LACNOG para impulsar estándares que reconozcan dominios y correos en cualquier idioma, asegurando que la transformación digital de América Latina llegue a todos.

item.title: Semana del Día de Internet
item.description: Del 18 al 23 de mayo de 2026, coordinamos una serie de eventos conmemorativos del Día de Internet en diversas sedes de la ciudad, incluyendo universidades y centros de eventos, abordando temáticas clave como Seguridad, Gobernanza y Conectividad.

item.title: Celebración del 30 Aniversario del Capítulo México
item.description: La celebración del trigésimo aniversario del Capítulo México de Internet Society reunirá a sus pioneros para recordar el origen del primer backbone de internet mexicano y reconocer su trayectoria y aportes.

item.title: Lenguajes de Programación de Computadoras
item.description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción clara y estructurada a los fundamentos de los lenguajes de programación, dirigido a quienes están interesados en comprender cómo funcionan las herramientas que impulsan el desarrollo de software.

item.title: Uso Correcto de la Tecnología Digital
item.description: Este seminario ofrece una introducción práctica y accesible sobre cómo utilizar la tecnología digital de forma segura, responsable y eficiente.

item.title: IA para Planificación y Diseño de Redes- Junio
item.description: El curso tiene 4 secciones donde aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.

item.title: Fundamentos de IA Aplicada a Redes- Mayo
item.description: Este curso se divide en 4 secciones. Aprenderás a aplicar IA en redes, analizar demanda con datos geoespaciales, optimizar despliegues y liderar la adopción tecnológica.

item.title: Universal Acceptance Day 2026- Regional Event
item.description: Register Idea Universal Acceptance is a global initiative focused on promoting the internationalization of national domain names (IDNs) and the email addresses associated with them (EAIs).

item.title: South School on Internet Governance 2026
item.description: The South School on Internet Governance trains future thought leaders in Latin America and the Caribbean, preparing students to engage in international forums and shape the future of the internet in the region.

item.title: What does Digital Sovereignty mean for the Civil Society?
item.description: ISOC Switzerland Chapter’s yearly Public Policy Sessions event will be dedicated in 2026 on the concept of digital sovereignty, launching a long term campaign asking the question “What does digital sovereignty mean?” from the perspective of different actors. As a US-based organization defending digital rights across the world since the 90s, the Internet Society’s European Chapters can play a key role in making sure that the current wave of European claims on digital sovereignty will strengthen digital rights worldwide, while taking into account important challenges related to ecological sustainability, health, and resilience and key values like openness, privacy, and democracy. We are proud to welcome in Zurich 5 special guests with strong expertise on this topic from an academic and/or policy perspective, and active engagement with various civil society organizations, like ISOC Germany and Serbia, FSFE, Wikimedia, La Quadrature du Net. You will have also the chance to meet the growing ISOC Switzerland Chapter and its new home at the center of Zurich, the L200 space, and participate in the discussion!

item.title: Art by the Internet
item.description: A talk by DY Kim

item.title: Meta-Layer Design Challenge
item.description: The Meta-Layer Design Challenge is a global, week-long design challenge exploring how learning, collaboration, and collective intelligence can be supported above the existing Web through a shared civic interface layer - the Meta-Layer. Rather than building new platforms, participants are invited to design concepts, interfaces, and governance models that operate across websites using overlays, shared context, trust signals, and community coordination. The challenge emphasizes human-centered design, interoperability, and public-interest outcomes. The Design Challenge is part of Meta-Layer Week (January 21–28, 2025), co-hosted with Learning Planet. Activities include live sessions, workshops, demonstrations, office hours, and a final showcase. Participants may submit conceptual designs, mockups, diagrams, governance proposals, or lightweight prototypes. No coding is required. Both a Student Track and an Open Track are offered, with shared judging criteria focused on creativity, feasibility, and relevance to collective learning and public-interest infrastructure. The challenge is relevant to Internet governance, digital public infrastructure, education, and community-driven innovation, and aligns with long-standing Internet Society interests in an open, trustworthy, and people-centered Internet. Audience: Students, educators, designers, technologists, researchers, and community organizers

item.title: Budding Polymath
item.description: An intra-chapter event which showcases our young talent presenting on relevant topics

item.title: LINUX Para Uso Cotidiano
item.description: Este seminario introduce a Linux como una herramienta útil para el uso cotidiano. Se presentará el trasfondo operativo del sistema, las diferencias entre diversas distribuciones y cómo obtenerlas e instalarlas en distintas plataformas. Incluye demostraciones prácticas del entorno Linux y una sesión interactiva de preguntas y respuestas para aclarar dudas y guiar a quienes deseen comenzar a utilizar este sistema operativo en su vida diaria.

item.title: AI SIG "Wire-Free Sewer Vision" Hybrid Hackathon
item.description: A critical event designed to challenge how we monitor and maintain underground sewer systems using cutting-edge, autonomous technology.

item.title: Universal Acceptance on a Multilingual Internet
item.description: This webinar will explore how Universal Acceptance forms the foundation for a truly multilingual Internet, the challenges of regional language support, multilingual DNS, inclusion in digital platforms, and the transformative role of technology, design, and policy in shaping linguistic equity online.

item.title: ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize
item.description: Delivery of the ISOC PT 2025 Internet Research Prize and panel discussion on the Age Control proposals

item.title: UK Internet Governance Forum 2025
item.description: One-day event in London focused on “Building Digital Governance Together,” exploring key issues like digital cooperation, trust, innovation, and universal digital rights to shape the future of internet governance in the UK

item.title: How to Find Funders and Partners
item.description: Webinar organized by Ghana chapter to educate others on how to find funders and partners for their activities.
